user_query:
I'm a high school basketball coach in Japan and want to automate a weekly "Season Flashback" note for my players that celebrates last season with a warm, nostalgic tone.

Please build a small, local workflow that I can schedule weekly. Use the provided input files at input/ to generate three outputs under output/ and set up a cron-style schedule file. Then run it once now to produce the first set of artifacts.

Inputs to use:
- input/season_stats.csv (game-by-game results)
- input/diary.md (dated coaching diary excerpts)

Deliverables to create:
1) output/report.md — A concise season recap that you compose from the inputs with:
   - A title and short intro in a reflective, nostalgic tone.
   - "By the numbers" section that includes: total games, wins, losses, total points for, total points against, and average points for and against (rounded to one decimal).
   - "Top 3 games by point differential" section listing each item with: date (YYYY-MM-DD), opponent, Home/Away, score (PF–PA), point differential, and (if that date exists in input/diary.md) include one short quote from the diary (≤ 15 words) in quotation marks after the line. If a top game’s date is not in the diary, omit the quote for that item.
   - Use only data from input/season_stats.csv and input/diary.md to compute and populate these fields.

2) output/email_draft.txt — A team email you draft that:
   - Opens with "Team," and a warm, respectful greeting.
   - Summarizes the season using the computed record (W–L) and references the three top-differential games.
   - Has a clear, positive closing about what the season meant to us.

3) output/line_short.txt — A rewrite of the above email for a shorter LINE post that:
   - Is ≤ 120 words, preserves the record and at least two of the top games, and uses a warm, nostalgic tone.
   - Prioritizes clarity and brevity while retaining the key facts.

4) output/run_log.json — A JSON log capturing:
   - ISO-8601 timestamp of the run in Asia/Tokyo time.
   - SHA256 checksums of input/season_stats.csv and input/diary.md and of each of the three outputs created.
   - The computed W and L totals and the list of the three top games by date for verification.

Scheduling requirement:
- Create schedule/nostalgia.cron containing a cron-compatible entry that runs at 08:00 every Monday in Asia/Tokyo (use a CRON_TZ or equivalent line). The command in the cron entry must call a local runner script at schedule/run_flashback.sh in this workspace.
- Create schedule/run_flashback.sh that, when executed from the repo root, regenerates the above outputs using input/season_stats.csv and input/diary.md.

Execution for verification:
- After implementing, run schedule/run_flashback.sh once now so that output/report.md, output/email_draft.txt, output/line_short.txt, and output/run_log.json exist and reflect the provided inputs.

Notes:
- Keep everything self-contained; do not send any emails or use external services.
- All computed values must come from the provided inputs so they can be re-checked from the artifacts.
- Make the report and messages genuinely nostalgic about the past season, but grounded in the stats and diary excerpts.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
